title: diplotaxis crassifolia subsp. intricata
intro: Diplotaxis crassifolia subsp. intricata, also known as the lesser or common wall rocket, is a perennial plant species within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its edible leaves and flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress and infections. The leaves and flowers of Diplotaxis crassifolia subsp. intricata can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Additionally, the plant is a rich source of vitamins and minerals, making it a nutritious addition to salads and other dishes.
